purpose: Seeded personal-knowledge corpus + query set with gold labels for the hybrid-vs-vector-vs-BM25 A/B over the production knowledge-bank retrieval path (cosine + FTS5 BM25, RRF k=60). Each corpus doc is short enough to be a single chunk, so doc-level relevance = chunk-level and recall@k is unambiguous. Queries are tagged by the limb they are designed to stress: `vector` (paraphrase / synonym / zero rare-keyword overlap - the semantic limb should win, BM25 should miss), `lexical` (exact rare token: part number, crypto term, proper name, domain - BM25 should win, the vector limb may misrank alphanumerics), `neutral` (natural keyword + semantic overlap - both limbs should work). The A/B question the study poses: does RRF fusion recover the per-limb wins so the fused ranker dominates either limb alone on a mixed query workload.

corpus.title: Home file server
corpus.text: My network-attached storage box is named Vault and lives at 192.168.1.42. It runs TrueNAS Scale and holds the household backups and the media library.

corpus.title: Home router
corpus.text: The home router is a Ubiquiti Dream Machine. Its admin page is at 192.168.1.1 and the main Wi-Fi network is called Nightingale.

corpus.title: Car servicing
corpus.text: The Subaru Outback is due for an oil change every 6,000 miles. The last service was at 78,400 miles in April.

corpus.title: Passport
corpus.text: My passport expires in March 2029. The renewal takes about six weeks by mail, so I should start the paperwork by January.

corpus.title: Pour-over coffee
corpus.text: I brew pour-over with a one-to-sixteen ratio, thirty grams of medium-roast beans to four hundred eighty grams of water, bloomed for forty-five seconds.

corpus.title: Evening prescription
corpus.text: I take ten milligrams of atorvastatin every evening with dinner. The prescription refills at the pharmacy on Elm Street.

corpus.title: Allergies
corpus.text: I am allergic to shellfish and to penicillin. Both reactions are severe, so they belong on every medical form.

corpus.title: Flight seating
corpus.text: On flights I always book an aisle seat near the front so I can stretch my legs and leave the cabin quickly.

corpus.title: Anniversary
corpus.text: Our wedding anniversary is on the twelfth of September. I usually book dinner at the Italian place on Prince Street.

corpus.title: Mortgage
corpus.text: The mortgage is with First Federal at a fixed 4.25 percent rate. The monthly payment is due on the first of each month.

corpus.title: Workstation hardware
corpus.text: The workstation has an Intel Arc 140V integrated GPU on a Lunar Lake chip with thirty-two gigabytes of shared LPDDR5X memory.

corpus.title: Rebuilding the dev environment
corpus.text: To rebuild the project environment, delete the virtualenv folder and run the bootstrap script, which pins every dependency by hash.

corpus.title: Branch discipline
corpus.text: We never commit straight to the main branch. Every change goes on a feature branch and merges only after the review gate passes.

corpus.title: Encryption at rest
corpus.text: All the personal stores are encrypted at rest with a hardware-sealed key envelope, so the files are useless if the disk is stolen.

corpus.title: Backup schedule
corpus.text: Backups run every night at two in the morning to the Vault box, and a monthly copy goes to an off-site encrypted drive.

corpus.title: Houseplants
corpus.text: The fiddle-leaf fig by the window needs watering once a week; the succulents on the shelf only every two or three weeks.

corpus.title: Dentist
corpus.text: My dentist is Dr. Okafor on Maple Avenue. Cleanings are every six months; the next one is booked for October.

corpus.title: Guest Wi-Fi
corpus.text: The guest wireless password is taped inside the kitchen cabinet door above the kettle. It rotates every few months.

corpus.title: Risotto method
corpus.text: For weeknight risotto, toast the arborio rice, add warm stock a ladle at a time, and finish with parmesan and a knob of butter.

corpus.title: Running routine
corpus.text: I run three times a week, usually a five-kilometre loop around the reservoir, and a longer ten-kilometre run on Sunday mornings.

corpus.title: Retirement account
corpus.text: The retirement account is a low-cost index fund tracking the total world market, rebalanced once a year in December.

corpus.title: Reading list
corpus.text: I am reading through the Culture novels by Iain Banks; after that I want more hard science fiction with a strong plot.

corpus.title: Listening habits
corpus.text: For focused work I put on ambient or modern classical; while cooking I prefer upbeat jazz or bossa nova.

corpus.title: French practice
corpus.text: I practise French for fifteen minutes every morning with flashcards, aiming to hold a conversation by next summer.

corpus.title: Office printer
corpus.text: The office printer is a Brother laser model; it uses TN-760 toner cartridges, and there is a spare in the supply closet.

corpus.title: Car insurance
corpus.text: The car insurance policy renews in June through Kestrel Mutual; the policy number starts with KM and eight digits.

corpus.title: Emergency contact
corpus.text: In an emergency, call my sister Nadia first; her number is under the contact 'Sis' and she keeps a spare house key.

corpus.title: Thermostat schedule
corpus.text: The smart thermostat drops to eighteen degrees Celsius overnight and warms back to twenty-one by the time the alarm goes off.

corpus.title: Server login
corpus.text: The server login uses an ed25519 SSH key stored on the hardware token; password logins are disabled entirely.

corpus.title: Autumn trip
corpus.text: We are planning a trip to Portugal in the autumn, two weeks split between Lisbon and the Algarve coast.

corpus.title: Taxes
corpus.text: I file taxes in April and keep every receipt in a labelled folder; the accountant is the same firm as last year, Brandt and Cole.

corpus.title: Gift ideas for Dad
corpus.text: For Dad's birthday he mentioned wanting a good cast-iron skillet and a subscription to a woodworking magazine.

corpus.title: Travel laptop
corpus.text: The travel laptop is a fanless ARM machine with an eighteen-hour battery, which is why I take it on long flights.

corpus.title: Gym
corpus.text: My gym membership is at the Riverside club; it is quietest on weekday mornings before eight, which is when I lift weights.

corpus.title: Personal domain
corpus.text: I own the domain ducray.dev through a registrar with privacy protection; it auto-renews every year in November.

queries.query: which medicine do I take for cholesterol?
queries.note: atorvastatin is a cholesterol drug; the words 'cholesterol'/'medicine' never appear - pure semantic, BM25 blind

queries.query: how do I get my coding setup working again?
queries.note: 'rebuild the project environment' - no shared rare keyword

queries.query: what seat do I prefer on planes?
when should I renew my travel document?
what foods are unsafe for me to eat?
how cold does the house get overnight?
who do I reach out to if something goes wrong?
what should play while I make dinner?
TN-760 toner cartridge
queries.note: exact part number - BM25 nails it, embeddings misrank alphanumerics

queries.query: ed25519 SSH key
Kestrel Mutual policy
Dr. Okafor appointment
Brandt and Cole accountant
ducray.dev registrar renewal
TrueNAS Scale Vault
Ubiquiti Dream Machine Nightingale
when is my next dentist cleaning?
how often do I water the fiddle-leaf fig?
what interest rate is my mortgage?
wedding anniversary date
where is the guest wifi password?
miles between oil changes on the Subaru
what GPU is in the workstation?
nightly backup schedule to the Vault
queries.note: backup/Vault exact; d01 is a strong distractor (also mentions Vault + backups)
